title: FRIDAY - AI Personal Assistant Web App

requirements.text: Create a JARVIS-inspired dark UI theme for FRIDAY with a sleek, futuristic aesthetic: deep black/dark charcoal backgrounds, glowing amber/gold accent colors, clean sans-serif typography, animated HUD-style elements (circular audio visualizer, pulsing orb for active state), and a responsive two-panel layout (sidebar for history/settings, main chat area). Apply this theme consistently across all components.

quotes: FRIDAY should behave like a smart assistant

acceptanceCriteria: Dark background (#0a0a0f or similar) with gold/amber accents (#f5a623 or similar)
Animated pulsing orb or circular visualizer visible when FRIDAY is active or speaking
Sidebar displays conversation history and basic settings
Layout is responsive and works on desktop and tablet viewports
All text is legible with sufficient contrast ratios

requirements.text: Build the FRIDAY chat interface with a full-screen chat window that displays conversation messages, differentiating between user messages and FRIDAY's responses with distinct styling. Include a text input field with a send button and a microphone button for voice input. Display FRIDAY's 'thinking' animation while processing a response.

quotes: natural conversational style like two humans speaking

acceptanceCriteria: User messages appear right-aligned; FRIDAY responses appear left-aligned with FRIDAY avatar/icon
A typing/thinking animation is shown while awaiting a response
Text input supports Enter key to send
Microphone button toggles voice input mode with visual indicator
Chat history scrolls automatically to the latest message

requirements.text: Implement browser-based voice interaction using the Web Speech API: speech recognition (SpeechRecognition) for converting user voice to text, and speech synthesis (SpeechSynthesis) for FRIDAY to speak responses aloud. Include a visual audio waveform/pulse animation while FRIDAY is speaking. Support toggling voice output on/off.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the microphone button starts voice recognition and transcribes speech to the input field
FRIDAY's text responses are read aloud via SpeechSynthesis when voice mode is enabled
A visual indicator (pulsing animation or waveform) is shown while FRIDAY is speaking
Voice output can be toggled on/off via a settings control
Microphone permission request is handled gracefully with a user-facing error message if denied

requirements.text: Implement a wake word detection simulation in the frontend: when the user types or says 'Hey Friday', FRIDAY activates from an idle state, plays a brief activation sound or animation, and acknowledges with a greeting. Include an idle/active state visual distinction (e.g., dimmed orb when idle, glowing orb when active).
acceptanceCriteria: Typing 'Hey Friday' or similar trigger phrase activates FRIDAY from idle state
Idle state shows a dimmed/static orb; active state shows a glowing/animated orb
FRIDAY responds with a greeting message upon activation
State persists for the duration of the session until manually deactivated

requirements.text: Implement multilingual support in the frontend UI. Allow users to select their preferred language from English, Telugu, and Hindi via a language selector in the settings panel. Store the selected language preference and display FRIDAY's UI labels and placeholder text in the chosen language. Show a language badge in the interface indicating the current active language.

quotes: FRIDAY must speak and understand multiple languages including: English, Telugu, Hindi

acceptanceCriteria: Language selector dropdown is accessible from the settings sidebar
UI labels (e.g., input placeholder, button tooltips) update to reflect selected language
Selected language is persisted in localStorage across sessions
Current language is visibly indicated in the UI (e.g., a flag icon or language badge)
SpeechSynthesis voice is set to match the selected language when available

requirements.text: Build an intelligent command parser in the frontend that interprets user input and maps it to one of FRIDAY's capability categories: System Control (open app, volume, screenshot), File Management (search files, list files), Web Search (search the web for X), Communication (send email, read email), Content Creation (write email, create presentation, code snippet), and General Conversation. Display the detected intent as a small tag/badge on FRIDAY's response card.
acceptanceCriteria: User input is parsed client-side using keyword and pattern matching to detect intent category
Each FRIDAY response card displays a small badge (e.g., 'WEB SEARCH', 'SYSTEM', 'CREATIVE') indicating the resolved intent
At least 6 intent categories are supported with distinct response templates
Unrecognized inputs fall back gracefully to a general conversation response
Intent detection logic is modular and easily extensible

requirements.text: Implement a task execution response system: for each detected intent category, FRIDAY provides a structured, context-aware response. For system control commands, FRIDAY responds with what it would do and provides OS-level instructions or simulation feedback. For web search, FRIDAY provides a simulated search summary. For content creation (emails, code, presentations), FRIDAY generates and displays the content inline in a formatted code/text block within the chat.
acceptanceCriteria: Code generation responses are displayed in a styled code block with syntax highlighting
Email drafts are displayed in a structured card with To/Subject/Body fields
System control responses describe the action taken and any simulated output
Web search responses provide a structured summary with simulated results
All response types are visually distinct and easy to read

requirements.text: Build a Motoko backend actor that stores user conversation history, user preferences (language, voice settings, name), and session data. Expose stable storage using a Trie or HashMap keyed by user principal. Provide query and update functions for: saving a message, retrieving conversation history (paginated), saving/reading user preferences, and clearing history.
acceptanceCriteria: saveMessage(role: Text, content: Text, timestamp: Int) : async () stores a message
getHistory(limit: Nat) : async [Message] returns the last N messages
savePreferences(prefs: UserPreferences) : async () persists user settings
getPreferences() : async ?UserPreferences retrieves stored preferences
clearHistory() : async () removes all stored messages for the caller
State survives canister upgrades via stable variables

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity authentication for FRIDAY. Only an authenticated user can access the assistant. Show a branded FRIDAY login screen with the logo and an 'Authenticate with Internet Identity' button before the chat is accessible. After login, display the user's principal ID in the settings sidebar. Add a logout button that clears the session.

quotes: Only follow commands from the authorized user

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users see only the FRIDAY login screen and cannot access the chat
Clicking 'Authenticate' triggers Internet Identity login flow
After successful authentication, the user is taken to the main FRIDAY chat interface
User's principal is displayed in the settings sidebar
Logout button ends the session and returns to the login screen
Backend functions are principal-scoped so each user only accesses their own data

requirements.text: Create a settings sidebar panel accessible via a gear icon. The panel should include: language selector (English/Telugu/Hindi), voice output toggle, voice speed/pitch sliders, user display name input (saved to backend), conversation history viewer with a 'Clear History' button, and an 'About FRIDAY' section displaying version and capabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings panel slides in from the left or right when the gear icon is clicked
Language selector, voice toggle, and speed/pitch controls are functional and update behavior immediately
Display name is saved to the backend on blur/enter and shown in the chat header
Clear History button calls the backend clearHistory function and empties the local chat display
About section lists FRIDAY's version, supported languages, and key capabilities

requirements.text: Integrate conversation history from the backend into the chat on login. On authentication, fetch the last 50 messages from the backend and render them in the chat window, clearly marked with timestamps. New messages sent during the session must be saved to the backend in real time via update calls.

quotes: Memory system using SQLite and ChromaDB for conversation history

acceptanceCriteria: On login, getHistory(50) is called and results are rendered in the chat
Each message shows a formatted timestamp
Every new user message and FRIDAY response is saved to the backend via saveMessage
History loads without blocking the UI (async with loading indicator)
Messages are displayed in chronological order

requirements.text: Add a quick-action command palette accessible via a '/' prefix in the chat input or a dedicated button. Display a grid of pre-built command shortcuts such as: 'Search Web', 'Write Email', 'Write Code', 'System Info', 'Translate', 'Set Reminder', 'Tell a Joke', 'Weather'. Clicking a shortcut pre-fills the input with a prompt template for that command.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing '/' in the input field opens the command palette overlay
At least 8 command shortcuts are displayed in a grid with icons and labels
Clicking a shortcut fills the input field with a template prompt
The palette can be dismissed by pressing Escape or clicking outside
A dedicated palette button is visible in the input toolbar

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo)
No external AI API integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) — AI responses must be simulated client-side via pattern matching and template responses
No Python, Rust, or server-side code — the system must be implemented entirely in Motoko (backend) and React/TypeScript (frontend)
No real device/OS control — system control commands must be simulated with descriptive responses explaining what FRIDAY would do
No real email sending — email commands generate draft content only
No WebSockets or real-time connections — use polling or canister query calls
Use only Web Speech API for voice (no third-party voice SDKs)
Authentication must use Internet Identity only
nonGoals: Real OS/device control or system-level automation
Integration with external AI models (GPT-4, Whisper, etc.)
Real email sending or calendar integration
Mobile app or React Native implementation
Wake word detection via Picovoice or any native binary — browser text matching only
Python implementation or any backend language other than Motoko

required: FRIDAY AI assistant logo — a glowing golden hexagonal emblem with the letter 'F' in the center and subtle circuit-line details radiating outward on a transparent background (friday-logo.dim_256x256.png)
FRIDAY login screen background — a deep black space-like background with faint golden geometric grid lines and soft glowing nodes, futuristic HUD aesthetic (friday-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
FRIDAY avatar icon — a sleek golden AI orb with a soft inner glow and subtle ring orbiting it, suitable as a chat message avatar (friday-avatar.dim_128x128.png)
